Privileged & Strictly Confidential 
 
U N I VER  SI T Y F EES  LI T I G A T I O N 
 
 
Summary Document 
 
 
This document is intended to be used as a 
summary of the claims we are proposing to bring 
and of the no -win, no-fee terms on which we are 
proposing to act.  It does not replace or change 
the terms of the agreements you will need to enter 
into with us in order for us to act for you.  You 
should read and consider those agreements 
carefully before deciding to join the claim.

carefully before deciding to join the claim. 
This document is strictly confidential and 
privileged. It should only be read by people who 
are genuinely considering instructing Harcus 
Parker Limited and Asserson Law Offices 
(referred jointly throughout this summary as ‘us’ or 
‘we’ or the ‘Solicitors’) in the University Fees 
Litigation. 
The claims 
1. We are bringing claims on behalf of current 
and former students of universities and other 
higher education institutions in respect of t he

higher education institutions in respect of t he 
disruption to their studies and course or 
courses related to industrial action from 2018 
onwards and/or the responses of their 
educational institutions to Covid-19 from 2020 
onwards (the ‘ University Fees Litigation’). 
2. The claims will comprise different legal causes 
of action, which could include: 
a. Breaches of contract by a 
university/higher education 
institution; and/or 
b. Breaches of consumer protection

b. Breaches of consumer protection 
laws by a university/higher education 
institution 
Because you have been charged significant 
fees by the university or by another institution 
in exchange for teaching and other services 
but a portion of what you paid for has simply 
not been provided to you or has been 
provided to you in a form substantially 
different from what you bargained for. 
3. We shall seek  financial compensation 
(damages) from the university on your behalf.

(damages) from the university on your behalf. 
The terms on which we would act for you 
4. Everybody who instructs us to act for them in 
the claims must agree to an Authority 
Document and to our Engagement Letter, 
which includes a number  of important 
schedules. These are: 
a. a damages-based agreement (which 
you will often see shortened to ‘DBA’); 
b. a litigation management agreement (or 
‘LMA’); and 
c. The firms’ privacy notice. 
5. In summary, these documents create a no-win,

5. In summary, these documents create a no-win, 
no-fee relationship between us and you, under 
which we agree to act for you in your claim in 
return for a percentage of any damages you 
receive. We also agree to pay up front all of 
the expenses associated with the case on your 
behalf. 
The damages-based agreement 
6. The DBA governs the way in which we will be 
paid for the work that we do on your behalf. 
We will be paid 35% (inclusive of any 
applicable VAT) of any damages, settlement

applicable VAT) of any damages, settlement 
monies, or benefit in kind you receive 
(although this should be reduced by any 
solicitors’ and  barristers’ fees we are able to 
claim back from the defendant). 
7. The DBA also provides that we will pay on your 
behalf expenses required to bring the claim  
(for example, the fees of experts or court fees). 
You will only be liable to pay us that money if 
we are able to recover it from the defendant. It 
is an additional amount to the 35% mentioned

is an additional amount to the 35% mentioned 
in paragraph 6 above. 
8. If your claim succeeds and you pay a fee to us, 
Harcus Parker Limited and Asserson  Law 
Offices will split that fee equally, and also 
share it with any third -party funders and 
insurers who support the claims. 
The LMA and Authority Document 
9. The LMA sets out how the claim will be run: it 
deals with things like the way confidential 
information can be shared between the 
claimants; the establishment of a

claimants; the establishment of a 
representative committee of claimants who will 
give us day -to-day instructions on the claim 
(the ‘ Committee’); the way costs will be 
shared between you and the other claimants; 
and the  way that the hypothetical risk of 
adverse costs is shared between you and the 
other claimants. 
10. The Committee is an important feature of the 
way in which these claims are run:  it is made 
up of members of the claimant group  who are

up of members of the claimant group  who are 
interested and wish to be more engaged in the 
litigation process. If you are interested in 
joining the Committee, please write to 
info@studentgroupclaim.co.uk, after you 
have registered as a

Confidential & Privileged 
2 
 
 
 
 
claimant. Through the LMA and the Authority 
Document, you agree that the Committee can 
take certain decisions in relation to your claim 
on your behalf. Importantly, this includes 
decisions as to settlement of your claim out-of- 
court. The Committee is necessary in claims of 
this sort so that we are able to take instructions 
quickly and efficiently from a large group of 
individual claimants. 
Other matters 
11. In addition, Harcus Parker Limited and

11. In addition, Harcus Parker Limited and 
Asserson Law Offices have agreed to 
cooperate with each other pursuant to the 
terms of a cooperation agreement. The firms 
recognise that it is in the best interests of their 
clients for them to cooperate in relation to the 
proceedings and have agreed to sh are the 
work required to bring the claims. This does 
not affect or change the no -win, no -fee 
relationship between you and the firms as 
outlined above.

outlined above. 
12. If the Committee instructs us to do so, we may 
establish a joint committee of claimants from 
other claimant groups pursuing similar or 
related claims so that decisions in respect of 
strategy can be made on a basis that ensures 
the smooth running of the  claims. The  LMA 
also provides that certain costs which will be 
common to groups of claimants will be shared. 
This should mean cost-savings overall, and will 
allow us to bring the claims more efficiently. 
Cost sharing

Cost sharing 
13. The LMA governs the way costs are shared 
between you and the other claimants, and 
between you, and if necessary or appropriate, 
claimants wi th similar claims against 
defendants other than  your university . 
Although our fee will be calculated by 
reference to the amount of damages  you 
receive, it is important – at least notionally – 
for us to divide between all the claimants the 
costs we incur on your behalf. This is so that 
at the conclusion of the  case, if your claims

at the conclusion of the  case, if your claims 
succeed, you are able to recover some  of 
your costs from the defendant (this is because 
a court will only award costs to claimants 
where they have in fact incurred  costs, or a 
costs liability). 
14. Your hypothetical share of the costs will be 
calculated by reference to the damages value 
of your claim. This way each claimant is liable 
only for his or her pro rata share of the total 
amount of costs. 
Risks, indemnity and insurance

amount of costs. 
Risks, indemnity and insurance 
15. Claimants who become involved in a court 
case put themselves at risk of being ordered, 
 
or having to agree, to pay the defendant’s 
costs if they lose their claim or a court 
application. 
16. In your case, we have arranged with a litigation 
funder for  it to pay for adverse costs orders 
that could be made against you at an early 
stage of your case. This indemnity is up to a 
financial limit that is spread across various

financial limit that is spread across various 
cases against universities. Please do contact 
us on info@studentgroupclaim.co.uk if y ou 
would like more details. We shall keep your 
Committee advised and apprised of the 
situation in relation to adverse costs cover for 
you, including before we take any steps that 
could expose you to having to pay adverse 
costs. It is also possible that aft er the event 
(‘ATE’) insurance may need to be sought at 
some stage in your case to cover you in

some stage in your case to cover you in 
relation to future adverse costs orders against 
you, although again we will inform you of the 
situation in this regard before it could arise. We 
would only act on your instructions in relation 
to the above, through your Committee. 
17. In addition, we will seek an order from the court 
which will ensure that the risk will be shared 
proportionately between the claimants, based 
upon their percentage share of the overall

upon their percentage share of the overall 
damages being claimed. This means that if the 
case is unsuccessful, and there was an 
insufficient indemnity in place, your liability will 
be limited to your proportionate share of the 
defendants’ costs. 
18. It is our duty to minimise  the risk to you as far 
as possible. The indemnity should mean that 
the risk will be negligible. 
19. Nevertheless, there are some remaining risks. 
We describe the main risks and what we will 
do to mitigate them here.

do to mitigate them here. 
Insufficient indemnity 
20. The court’s case man agement processes 
mean that it is likely that the defendant will be 
obliged to update us regularly about its costs,

Confidential & Privileged 
3 
 
 
 
 
which will increase as the case progresses, so 
that we can take steps to ensure that the 
indemnity covering you is sufficient, and if it is 
backed by an insurance policy and limited to 
that amount, that such insurance is sufficient. 
However, if notwithstanding those protections 
the indemnity is insufficient to meet the 
adverse costs you are re quired to pay, you 
would be liable for the sum not covered by the 
indemnity. This is very unlikely to be the case.

indemnity. This is very unlikely to be the case. 
The group does not attract enough claimants 
21. There is also a further risk: that the case is 
successful but any recoveries for the claimants 
are limited. 
22. The terms of the DBA are such that we cannot 
take more than 35% of any damages you  
receive, but if too few claimants instruct us, so 
as to make the case not economically viable, 
we may have to terminate our retainer with 
you. This is unlikely, and we would consult the

you. This is unlikely, and we would consult the 
Committee before doing so in order to make 
sure the group could continue to be 
adequately represented, if at all possible. You 
would not be charged by us if we had to 
terminate your retainer for this reason. 
Our advice on risk 
23. It is our advice to potential claimants that the 
risk of personal liability falling on any  claimant 
is extremely low. 
Your responsibilities 
24. As a claimant, you will be a party to formal legal

proceedings. You must respond quickly and 
fully to any questions we ask you which relate 
to your claim. 
25. We will use the information that you provide to 
us as the basis of your claim. This will involve 
us making representations to the defendant 
and signing statements of truth on your behalf. 
It is essential that what we say  is true. If the 
contents of a statement of truth are not true 
contempt of court proceedings can be brought 
against you and us.

against you and us. 
26. You will also have a duty to disclose (that is, 
tell the other side about and provide copies of) 
documents which might be relevant to your 
claim regardless of whether they are harmful 
or helpful to your own case. This means that 
whilst you are a claimant you must keep such 
documents safe, and be prepared to produce 
them if required. 
Information sharing 
27. Confidential Information includes any 
information and documents provided by a

information and documents provided by a 
claimant to the firms for the purposes of the 
 
Claim save insofar as it is already in the public 
domain or requires to be disclosed to the court 
and the defendant or otherwise made public 
for the purposes of the Claim. 
28. Under the LMA you agree that the firms can 
share this information on a common interest 
basis with other claimants and any third-party 
funder who provides litigation funding for the 
claims and the Committee. You also agree

claims and the Committee. You also agree 
that Confidential Information can be used by 
the firms in the proceedings in so far as the 
firms consider it necessary or helpful to 
compare the facts of individual claims for the 
purposes of advising on and conducting the 
common aspects of the claimants’ claims and 
the claims of other clients of the firms with 
similar claims. In all cases Confidential 
Information will be shared on terms of mutual 
confidentiality and without any waiver of 
privilege.

privilege. 
Alternative arrangements 
29. You may have legal insurance whic h would 
pay your legal costs, typically as part of the 
household insurance policy. This is known as 
‘Before The Event’ (‘ BTE’) insurance. We 
regret that unfortunately you will not be able to 
use your BTE insurance for this claim; that is 
mainly because we do not think that it will be 
economically viable to assist claimants with 
individual insurance claims. 
30. Other law firms might be prepared to act for

30. Other law firms might be prepared to act for 
you in claims like these on a similar basis. We 
believe that the arrangements we have put in 
place are fair and in your best interests.  It is 
likely that a significant proportion of the claims 
will go to trial, and we have sought  to create a 
structure in which we can run that litigation in a 
way that is economically viable for us and for 
you. 
Cancellation 
31. If you change your mind after signing up, you

can cancel within 14 days without any reason. 
To do so, please contact Harcus Parker and 
Asserson by email at: 
info@studentgroupclaim.co.uk. We will email  
a cancellation form to you once you have 
completed the registration process. You may 
use this form to cancel, but you do not have to. 
Our engagement with you is governed by the 
Consumer Contracts (Information, 
Cancellation and Additional Charges) 
Regulations 2013.

 A U T H O R I T Y  D O C U M E N T  –  S T U D E N T  G R O U P  C L A I M 
 
_______________________________________ 
Authority Document

Authority Document 
_______________________________________ 
 
This is an authority document, which authorises Harcus Parker Limited and Asserson Law 
Offices to pursue the claim on my behalf. It also related to the following agreements: 
(A) The engagement letter from Harcus Parker Limited and Asserson Law Offices (together, 
the ‘Solicitors’) (the ‘Engagement Letter’);  
(B) The damages-based agreement (the ‘DBA’) between you and the Solicitors; and

(C) The litigation management agreement  (the ‘ LMA’) between you, each of the other 
claimants in your group claim, and the Solicitors. 
We are also making a summary document available to you here. 
You confirm and agree that: 
1. You have read in full and agree to the terms of each of the Engagement Letter, the DBA 
and the LMA; 
2. You are instructing Harcus Parker Limited and Asserson Law Offices to act as your 
solicitors for the purpose of your claim against your university;

3. We have informed you that o ur postal addresses are Harcus Parker Limited at 7th 
Floor, Melbourne House, 44 -46 Aldwych, London, WC2B 4LL  and Asserson Law 
Offices at Central Court, 25 Southampton Buildings, Holborn, London, WC2A 1AL, as 
well as Churchill House, 137-139 Brent Street, London, NW4 4DJ, and 1 Azrieli Centre, 
Round Tower, 32nd Floor, 132 Menachem Begin, Tel Aviv 6701101 . Both la w firms 
can however be contacted via email address info@studentgroupclaim.co.uk, which is

defendant for you;  
6. The litigation is of indeterminate duration. Your contract with us will terminate after your 
litigation concludes by way of a win or loss at court, or by achieving an out -of-court 
settlement, and after all legal services fees and expenses have been paid. If your case 
is contested by your university, the litigation is likely to last at l east two years but the

timetable is at the discretion of the Court . It is also dependent on the conduct of the 
parties; 
7. It is impossible for us to predict accurately what cost will be attributable for which month

during which the litigation will carry on.  In addition, th e nature of the case, where 
compensation rates are still to be decided,  is such that the specific price of our legal 
services cannot be calculated in advance;  
8. The fee we will charge you is conditional on our achieving a settlement or a judg ment 
in your favour. Our fee for our legal services will be, and cannot be more than , 35% 
(inclusive of VAT) of any damages (ie, compensation) , settlement monies, and the

market value of benefits in kind you receive from your university. This fee can however 
be reduced by solicitors’ and barristers’ fees we are able to claim back from your 
university as part of a settlement or a court award . The way in which the fee for our 
legal services is calculated is set out fully in the DBA;  
9. We will also pay on your behalf expenses required to pursue your claim (for example, 
the fees of experts and court fees). You will be liable to repay those expenses to us,

but only if and to the extent that you are reimbursed your expenses by your university. 
Reimbursement of those expenses is an additional amount to the payment referred to 
in paragraph 8 above. It should not leave you out of pocket because of the fact that 
expenses are only repayable to us if your university repays them to you first; 
10. The only fees and expenses payable by you to us are those set out in your 
Engagement Letter and the DBA. You must read those documents carefully before

agreeing to this Authority Document. There will be no additional legal services fees or 
expenses payable by you to us; 
11. You are giving to the Claimant Committee an irrevocable delegation of authority to 
conduct the litigation on your behalf. You may with their permission nevertheless 
discontinue your claim. The cost consequences of doing so are detailed in our 
complete info rmation sheet related to the Consumer Contracts (Information,

notify us in an email sent to info@studentgroupclaim.co.uk, although that method of 
communicating with us is not obligatory; 
14. If you cancel your agreement with us wit hin 14 days of the date of acceptance of our 
terms, we will not charge you anything. However, if during that period you have 
instructed us to issue a claim form at Court on your behalf, then there may be costs 
consequences of cancelling your agreement with  us (and discontinuing your claim at

including that there are risks to such litigation, and that by becoming a claimant you

are taking on certain obligations to the Court, to the Solicitors and to your fellow 
claimants; 
17. You understand that you have a duty to search for and to disclose documents that are 
relevant to your claim. You agree not to destroy any documents which are or might be 
relevant to your claim; 
18. A director of Harcus Parker Limited or a partner of Asserson Law Offices may sign a 
statement of truth in respect of a claim form and particulars of claim based on the

party liability that the Solicitors advise is necessary or expedient for 
the conduct of the litigation; and 
v) strategy generally; as well as

In such a situation, references to the Committee in this document shoul d be read as 
references to the Solicitors; 
26. Complaints about Harcus Parker Limited should be made in the first instance to 
Managing Partner Damon Parker. Complaints about Asserson Law Offices should be 
made in the first instance to Senior Partner Trevor Asserson. If you are not happy with 
the response you receive, you may apply to the Solicitors’ Regulation Authority (see:

necessary for you to be in touch with us from time to time in order to fulfil your duties 
as a Claimant in the litigation. 
You may cancel your agreement with us within 14 days without any reason.  To do so, 
please contact Harcus Parker and Asserson (ideally by email at:  
info@studengroupclaim.co.uk) and we will email a cancellation form to you. A 
cancellation form can also be found here. This agreement is governed by the Consumer

Schedule: Information Regarding t he Consumer Contracts (Information, Cancellation and 
Additional Charges) Regulations 2013 (the ‘Regulations’)

This Schedule sets out the information contained in Schedule 2 to the Regulations. 
 
a. the main characteristics of the goods, services or digital content to the extent appropriate to the 
medium of communication and to the goods, services or digital content; 
 
The services  that Harcus Parker Limited and Ass erson Law Offices are providing to you are legal 
services in relation to group litigation against your university, as described on the case website and in

the Engagement Letter, as well as its enclosures. We are not providing any goods or digital content. 
 
b. the identity of the trader (such as the trader’s trading name);  
 
Your retainer is with Harcus Parker Limited and Asserson Law Offices.  
 
We will also retain on your behalf other professionals such as barristers and, as appropriate, experts to 
help with the claim.  
 
c. the geographical address at which the trader is esta blished and, where available, the trader’s

telephone number, fax number and e-mail address, to enable the consumer to contact the trader quickly 
and communicate efficiently;  
 
- Harcus Parker Limited is at 7th Floor, Melbourne House, 44-46 Aldwych, London, WC2B 4LL 
- Asserson Law Offices is at Central Court, 25 Southampton Buildings, London WC2A 1AL 
 
It is important to the efficiency of how we run the claim that we attempt to limit the cost of dealing with

individual queries. We ask you to direct any queries that arise to info@studentgroupclaim.co.uk. 
 
d. where the trader is acting on behalf of another trader, the geographical address and identity of that 
other trader;

f. the total price of the goods, services or digital content inclusive of taxes, or where the nature of the

goods, services or digital content is such that the price cannot reasonably be calculated in advance, the 
manner in which the price is to be calculated;  
The nature of the services is such that the price cannot reasonably be calculated in advance. We have 
explained that our fee for our legal services will be, and cannot be more than, 35% (inclusive of VAT) 
of any damages (ie, compensation), settlement monie s, and the market value of benefits in kind you

receive from your university. This fee can however be reduced by solicitors’ and barristers’ fees we are 
able to claim back from your university as part of a settlement or a court award. The way in which the 
fee for our legal services is calculated is set out fully in the DBA. 
g. where applicable, all additional delivery charges and any other costs or, where those charges cannot
reasonably be calculated in advance, the fact that such additional charges may be payable;

We will also pay on your behalf expenses required to pursue your claim (for example, the fees of 
experts and court fees). You will be liable to repay those expenses to us, but only if and to the extent 
that you are reimbursed your expenses by your university. Reimbursement of those expenses is an 
additional amount to the payment referred to above. It should not leave you out of pocket because of 
the fact that expenses are only repayable to us if your university repays them to you first.

h. in the case of a contract of indeterminate duration or a contract containing a subscription, the totalcosts per 
billing period or (where such contracts are charged at a fixed rate) the total monthly costs; 
The litigation is of indeterminate duration. It is not possible for us to predict accurately what cost will 
be attributable for which month during which the litigation will carry on. Because, however, the costs

are conditional on success, we can say that there will be no month prior to the conclusion of the litigation 
in which you will be called on to make a payment to us.

As explained in the LMA, DBA, and the Engagement Letter, the arrangements for payment will be 
made from any settlement or judgment we achieve. 
We are able to give no undertaking as to how long it may take to conclude the litigation. If it is contested, 
the litigation is likely to last at least two years but the timetable is at the discretion of the Court and also 
is dependent on the conduct of the parties.

content; 
 
We do not provide digital content, but we collect and retain the information that you input into our 
website or otherwise communicate to us (for example by email or telephone), which may include 
contact information (including your email address and telephon e number) and information relevant to 
your enquiry or case. 
 
We may share your data in accordance with the LMA and the Engagement Letter, which means we

may, if we consider it to be necessary or helpful, share your data with the Committee, counsel, and other 
Claimants, as well as with other firms of solicitors with whom they may, with the approval of the 
Committee, subsequently also cooperate. 
 
If you provided us with an email address or mobile telephone number, we may send you emails or text 
messages about your enquiry or case. 
 
We will not sell, lease or otherwise distribute your personal information to third parties unless we have

your permission or are required by law to do so. However, we may share information with third parties 
whose services we use or whom we instruct on your behalf in connection with your case.

agencies and other authorities for regulatory reasons or for the prevention and detection of crime.

and regulatory reasons.
